# terrible_openstack_cloud_role_assignment (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `role` (String) Name or ID for the role.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `domain` (String) Name or ID of the domain to scope the role association to. Valid only with keystone version 3. Required if *project* is not specified. When *project* is specified, then *domain* will not be used for scoping the role association, only for finding resources. Deprecated for finding resources, please use *group_domain*, *project_domain*, *role_domain*, or *user_domain*. When scoping the role association, *project* has precedence over *domain* and *domain* has precedence over *system*: When *project* is specified, then *domain* and *system* are not used for role association. When *domain* is specified, then *system* will not be used for role association.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `group` (String) Name or ID for the group. Valid only with keystone version 3. If *group* is not specified, then *user* is required. Both may not be specified at the same time. You can supply *group_domain* or the deprecated usage of *domain* to find group resources.
- `group_domain` (String) Name or ID for the domain. Valid only with keystone version 3. Only valid for finding group resources.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `project` (String) Name or ID of the project to scope the role association to. If you are using keystone version 2, then this value is required. When *project* is specified, then *domain* will not be used for scoping the role association, only for finding resources. Prefer *group_domain* over *domain*. When scoping the role association, *project* has precedence over *domain* and *domain* has precedence over *system*: When *project* is specified, then *domain* and *system* are not used for role association. When *domain* is specified, then *system* will not be used for role association.
- `project_domain` (String) Name or ID for the domain. Valid only with keystone version 3. Only valid for finding project resources.
- `role_domain` (String) Name or ID for the domain. Valid only with keystone version 3. Only valid for finding role resources.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Should the roles be present or absent on the user.
- `system` (String) Name of system to scope the role association to. Valid only with keystone version 3. Required if *project* and *domain* are not specified. When scoping the role association, *project* has precedence over *domain* and *domain* has precedence over *system*: When *project* is specified, then *domain* and *system* are not used for role association. When *domain* is specified, then *system* will not be used for role association.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `user` (String) Name or ID for the user. If *user* is not specified, then *group* is required. Both may not be specified at the same time.
- `user_domain` (String) Name or ID for the domain. Valid only with keystone version 3. Only valid for finding user resources.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
